\vspace{0.4em}\par
\caption{Per-cell deltas in percentage points between this work (OURS, 5 seeds $\\times$ 10-fold, PCC+LR) and the paper's published numbers. Diagonal cells (target $=$ evaluation) agree within $\\sim 1$\\,pp on all tabular datasets and within $\\sim 0.1$\\,pp on chest-xray, confirming reproducibility. Two systematic off-diagonal differences (Markedness column, and Recall-target / Negative-eval pair) reflect a definition gap in $\\Fmar$ and a corner-case choice for $\\Fneg$ when the optimal predictor for $\\Frec$ outputs all~1's.}
\label{tab:ours-delta}
\end{table}

% =================== PCC vs BR head-to-head extension ===================
\begin{table}[!ht]\centering
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|r|}
\hline
Metric & PCC wins & Tie & BR wins & Mean (PCC$-$BR), pp \\
\hline
$\Fham$ & 11 & 7 & 15 & -0.40 \\
$\Fsub$ & 25 & 0 & 8 & -0.44 \\
$F_1$ & 20 & 5 & 8 & +0.51 \\
Avg.~Prec. & 16 & 2 & 15 & -1.80 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\caption{PCC vs.~Binary Relevance (BR) head-to-head across 33 (dataset $\times$ base estimator) cells per metric, using the inference rule that targets each metric. PCC wins decisively on structured-loss metrics ($\\Fsub$, $F_1$), while $\\Fham$ and ranking accuracy are essentially tied, consistent with $\\Fham$'s label-wise decomposability.}
\label{tab:pcc-vs-br}
\end{table}
